the_signal.headline: LLM routing moves from optimization hack to production necessity
the_signal.body: N8n's deep dive on per-request model selection shows routing is now table-stakes for controlling cost and latency in multi-model revenue systems. GTM engineers can't ship agent-heavy pipelines without strategic routing — it's the difference between sustainable and unsustainable inference budgets at scale.

tool_drop.name: N8n Process Orchestration Platform
tool_drop.what: Enterprise workflow engine that handles multi-agent coordination, LLM routing, and complex agent patterns in production.
tool_drop.why: If you're building agent-driven GTM systems, orchestration is where your actual reliability lives — it's the invisible layer preventing chaos when three agents need to talk to each other.

thread_of_day.title: Who Does What? Team Topologies for the Agentic Platform
thread_of_day.takeaway: Agentic platforms require new organizational models — your GTM ops team structure needs to reflect how agents distribute work, not how humans used to.

quote.text: Your first AI agent worked great. Then you added three more and the system became impossible to debug.
quote.source: N8n Blog — Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns

whats_moving.context: Multi-model selection per request is now essential for cost control in production revenue systems, not a nice-to-have optimization.
N8n's deep taxonomy of integration patterns signals the market is converging on standard approaches — GTM stacks need to standardize on these patterns.
Growing recognition that agent composition creates new failure modes; observability and orchestration become non-negotiable for revenue-critical systems.

act_on_this: Audit your current agent-driven GTM workflows for routing strategy — map which models run at which steps and calculate your actual monthly inference spend. You're likely overpaying on commodity tasks.

numbers.stat: n8n targeting 200-person UK team by 2029, tripling current size
numbers.context: Orchestration demand is global and growing fast; the infrastructure layer is consolidating around a few platforms, so choose your vendor lock-in carefully.
